title: viola bulbosa subsp. tuberifera
intro: Viola bulbosa subsp. tuberifera, also known as the tuberous violet, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the violet family (Violaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of China and Tibet. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine. The health benefits of Viola bulbosa subsp. tuberifera are attributed to its ability to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory disorders. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's tubers and roots are typically dried and processed into teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
